sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Betis have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with a strong 7-1 win against a lower-tier side but a loss to Atlético Madrid (0-2) and a draw against Villarreal (2-2). Mallorca, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last 3 matches, including a notable 3-1 away win over Sevilla, indicating improving form. Betis have struggled for consistency at home, while Mallorca’s recent performances suggest they can capitalize on defensive lapses.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, results have been split: Real Betis won 1-0 away in January 2025, while Mallorca secured a dramatic 2-1 victory at Betis’ ground in September 2024. Matches between these teams often see late goals and tight contests, with Betis historically taking the lead early in games. Head-to-head stats also indicate a trend of under 2.5 goals in 4 of the last 5 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Betis, sitting 7th with 16 points, have a solid offensive output (15 goals scored, 6th in La Liga) and a strong defense (12 goals conceded, tied 6th). Mallorca, positioned 16th with 9 points, have struggled offensively (11 goals) and defensively (15 conceded), but their recent away form shows resilience. Betis’ high possession (51.9%) and pressing stats (5.2 possessions won in final third per game) could dominate Mallorca’s lower possession stats.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Betis, Cucho Hernández (4 goals, 5th in La Liga) and Pablo Fornals (4 assists, 3rd in La Liga) are in fine form and crucial to their attack. Mallorca’s Abdon Prats, with 2 goals in the last 5 matches and a high Sofascore rating of 7.8, poses a threat up front. These players could be decisive in a tight encounter.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Real Betis’ superior league position, home advantage, and Sofascore’s 55% probability for a home win, they are favored to edge this contest. Mallorca’s recent form and ability to score late (5 goals in 76-90 minutes this season) could make it close, but Betis’ attacking firepower and defensive stability should prevail. Expect a low-scoring affair based on historical trends and current defensive stats.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half dominance by Real Betis
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis have taken a commanding lead in the first half at Benito Villamarin with a 3-0 scoreline against Mallorca. The home side showcased their clinical finishing, converting their opportunities with precision. Antony was the standout performer, netting a brace and providing an assist, while Abdessamad Ezzalzouli added to the tally with a well-taken goal. Betis maintained a slight edge in possession and made effective entries into the final third, capitalizing on their attacking momentum to secure a significant advantage at the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Mallorca's struggles in attack
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca, on the other hand, have struggled to make an impact in the final third despite having a fair share of possession and more penalty area entries. They failed to register a single shot on target in the first half and missed a big chance, highlighting their lack of sharpness upfront. Although they showed some intent with final third entries, their inability to convert these into meaningful chances has left them with a mountain to climb in the second half. A yellow card also adds to their disciplinary concerns.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw an initial surge from Real Betis as they pushed forward with intent early on. Mallorca responded with a sustained period of pressure, dominating the attacking play for a considerable stretch. However, Betis regained control midway through the half with a series of dangerous spells that ultimately led to their goals. Towards the end of the half, Betis continued to press forward with intent, while Mallorca's attacking efforts fizzled out, unable to maintain consistent pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers and tactical outlook
halftimeSections.text: Antony’s exceptional performance has been the difference for Real Betis, with his goal-scoring prowess and playmaking ability shining through. Support from players like Héctor Bellerín and Cucho Hernández in creating opportunities has also been vital. For Mallorca, players like Johan Mojica showed some creativity with key passes, but the lack of finishing from forwards such as Vedat Muriqi has been glaring. Tactically, Betis might look to manage the game in the second half, potentially sitting deeper to protect their lead, while Mallorca will need to push forward aggressively to salvage something from this match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the current scoreline and first-half dynamics, Real Betis are likely to adopt a more conservative approach in the second half, focusing on maintaining their lead while looking for counter-attacking opportunities. Mallorca, trailing by three goals, will have to take risks, potentially leaving spaces at the back. While Mallorca might create chances with their urgency, their lack of clinical finishing so far suggests they may struggle to score multiple goals. Betis, with their attacking threats still in play, could add to their tally if they exploit defensive gaps.
